message:
fix(net): allow cancel-before-running and completion-racing-cancel in worker job state machine

JobState transition table expanded to handle two real-world race
conditions in the worker coordinator protocol:

1. Queued → CancelRequested: a job can be cancelled before the worker
   sends a Running status snapshot. Previously this required the job to
   be Running first, forcing the coordinator to either reject valid
   cancellation requests or buffer them until a Running snapshot arrived
   (which might never come if the worker is slow or crashed).

2. CancelRequested → Completed: a job can complete naturally even after
   a cancel request was sent, if the worker finished the work before
   processing the cancellation. Previously this transition was rejected,
   forcing the coordinator through the full drain→finalize path even
   when the work was already done.

WorkerCoordinator::handle_inbound for JobCompleted now accepts jobs in
CancelRequested state in addition to Queued and Running.

The state diagram ASCII art in the doc comment is updated to show both
new edges.

Tests added:
- coordinator_accepts_job_completed_racing_with_cancel_request: spawns
  a job, advances to Running, sends cancel, then sends completion —
  verifies job reaches Completed with zero inflight
- coordinator_allows_cancel_before_running_snapshot: spawns a job,
  cancels while still Queued — verifies CancelRequested state and
  outbound CancelJob message
- Transition table assertions updated for the two new valid edges

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
